sets.passage: Most people share personal details online without giving the matter a second thought. Every single time we sign up for a new app or website, we hand over data that companies eagerly (1)____ for profit. These firms quietly build remarkably detailed profiles of our daily habits, and the practice has now become extraordinarily (2)____ across the entire technology industry. Very few users ever bother to read the lengthy privacy (3)____ that appear on the screen before installation, so they almost never understand what they have actually agreed to accept. As a direct result, sensitive information about ordinary individuals can easily be (4)____ to advertisers, insurers, employers, and even total strangers. Regulators in several countries have finally begun to respond, drafting fresh laws that (5)____ much stricter limits on how personal data may be gathered and stored. Yet enforcement still remains surprisingly weak, and the majority of violations go entirely (6)____ by the authorities. Critics reasonably argue that the heavy burden of protection should not fall on ordinary citizens, who clearly lack the technical (7)____ required to defend themselves. Instead, the companies that gather our records must finally be held fully (8)____ for the careless way they handle them. A handful of optimistic experts confidently (9)____ that future systems will eventually give individuals genuine control over their own information. Until that day arrives, the safest approach is simply to remain (10)____ , sharing only what is truly necessary and questioning every single request for access to our private lives. Awareness, after all, remains the very first line of defence in a connected world where our digital footprints quietly follow us everywhere we go.
sets.explain_zh: (1) collect 收集，宾语为 data，及物动词原形（前有 eagerly 修饰）；(2) widespread 普遍的，作表语形容词，搭配 across the industry；(3) agreements 协议，复数名词，privacy agreements 隐私协议；(4) exposed 被暴露，be exposed to sb 固定搭配；(5) impose 强加，impose limits on 固定搭配；(6) unnoticed 未被注意，go unnoticed 固定搭配作表语；(7) expertise 专业知识，不可数名词，technical expertise；(8) accountable 应负责的，be held accountable for 固定搭配；(9) predict 预测，后接 that 从句的及物动词；(10) cautious 谨慎的，作表语形容词。干扰项：generous(慷慨的)语义不合任何空；permanent(永久的)与各空搭配不当；reject(拒绝)与(9)的 that 从句语义相反，且(5)需 impose；loudly(大声地)副词无对应副词空；asleep(睡着的)为表语形容词，仅用于 fall/be asleep，与各空（动词/名词/表语 widespread、cautious 等语义场）均不搭配，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: Social media platforms are carefully designed to capture and hold our attention for as long as humanly possible. Endless scrolling feeds and brightly coloured notifications constantly (1)____ users back to their glowing screens, often dozens of separate times within a single hour. This steady stream of small interruptions makes it increasingly (2)____ for anyone to focus on a single demanding task for any length of time. Researchers across several universities have now noticed a measurable (3)____ in the average person's ability to concentrate over the past decade alone. Each fresh alert triggers a tiny burst of chemical pleasure, and the human brain quickly grows (4)____ to this small reward, soon craving the very next one. As a direct consequence, deep reading and slow, careful thought have become surprisingly (5)____ activities in modern daily life. Students in particular tend to (6)____ badly from this constant fragmentation, struggling to study for an hour without instinctively reaching for their phones. Some responsible companies now (7)____ helpful tools that quietly track screen time and gently remind people to take regular breaks. Many critics, however, seriously doubt whether such modest features can genuinely (8)____ the underlying problem at all. They point out that the entire business model itself (9)____ heavily on keeping us permanently distracted, since human attention is precisely what advertisers are willing to pay for. To reclaim our scattered minds, experts strongly suggest that we must (10)____ deliberately, switching off alerts and setting aside protected time for uninterrupted work. Only then, they insist, can our fragile concentration, once entirely taken for granted, slowly be rebuilt and restored to its former natural strength over time.
sets.explain_zh: (1) lure 引诱，lure sb back 及物动词原形（主语 feeds and notifications）；(2) difficult 困难的，makes it difficult for sb to 固定结构；(3) decline 下降，名词，a decline in ability；(4) addicted 上瘾的，grow addicted to 固定搭配；(5) rare 稀少的，作表语形容词修饰 activities；(6) suffer 受损，suffer from 固定搭配（后有 from）；(7) offer 提供，offer tools 及物动词原形；(8) address 解决，address the problem 及物动词；(9) depends 依赖，depend on 固定搭配（后有 on，主语第三人称单数加 s）；(10) act 行动，act deliberately 不及物动词原形。干扰项：easy(容易的)与(2)语义相反；patient(耐心的)无合适空；rise(上升)与(3)需 decline 语义相反；ignore(忽视)及物却无对应宾语空且语义不合；quickly(副词)无对应副词空。

sets.passage: The dramatic shift toward remote work has completely transformed how many millions of people now earn a living. When offices closed almost overnight during the pandemic, vast numbers of employees were suddenly forced to (1)____ to working from home with very little warning. To nearly everyone's genuine surprise, productivity in a great many roles actually (2)____ rather than falling, as critics had confidently feared. Workers happily saved long hours once wasted every day on the exhausting daily (3)____ , and many of them reported feeling noticeably less stressed than before. Companies, meanwhile, soon discovered that they could (4)____ significant sums of money simply by reducing their expensive office space. The benefits, however, are clearly not (5)____ across all professions; jobs that require hands-on physical tasks plainly cannot be performed at a distance. Critics also repeatedly warn that remote staff may gradually feel (6)____ from their colleagues, missing the casual hallway conversations that quietly build mutual trust. Maintaining a strong, united team (7)____ becomes very much harder when people only rarely meet one another in person. To solve this stubborn problem, many forward-thinking firms now adopt a (8)____ model, sensibly combining office days with home days. Managers must also patiently learn to (9)____ genuine output by measurable results rather than by the raw hours spent sitting at a desk. Although the whole transition has often been bumpy, very few serious experts now (10)____ that the world of work has changed permanently and for good. The traditional rigid nine-to-five office routine, once the unquestioned default for nearly everyone, may very well never fully return to the familiar way it once was before.
sets.explain_zh: (1) adapt 适应，adapt to 固定搭配（后有 to）；(2) rose 上升，过去式（与 falling 对比），不及物动词；(3) commute 通勤，名词，the daily commute；(4) save 节省，save sums 及物动词原形；(5) equal 均等的，作表语形容词，not equal across professions；(6) isolated 孤立的，feel isolated from 固定搭配；(7) spirit 精神，team spirit 团队精神（名词）；(8) hybrid 混合的，hybrid model 混合模式（形容词）；(9) measure 衡量，measure output 及物动词原形；(10) doubt 怀疑，few experts doubt that 及物动词。干扰项：fell(下降)与(2)语义相反；lonely(孤独的)虽近义但 feel lonely 不接 from，而(6)句末有 from 需 isolated；energy(精力)与 team 不搭配，team spirit 才地道；spend(花费)语义与 save 相反且(4)语境为节省；rapidly(副词)无对应副词空。

sets.passage: Electric vehicles are rapidly reshaping the way that modern cities think about transport and clean air. As governments around the world set increasingly ambitious targets to cut harmful emissions, sales of these quiet cars continue to (1)____ steadily year after year. Their growing (2)____ stems from one simple and powerful fact: they produce absolutely no exhaust at the point of use, which directly improves urban air quality for everyone. City planners are now racing to install enough charging stations, yet the supporting network unfortunately remains far from (3)____ in a great many residential neighbourhoods. Anxious drivers still worry about range, quietly fearing that their battery will (4)____ completely before they manage to reach the next charger. Manufacturers have responded by developing newer batteries that are both noticeably lighter and considerably more (5)____ , thereby extending the distance a single charge can comfortably cover. Critics, however, fairly point out that producing these batteries requires scarce rare metals, raising serious environmental (6)____ about mining and pollution. Moreover, the electricity used to charge the cars must itself eventually come from genuinely (7)____ sources if the promised benefits are ever to become real. Despite all these challenges, the cities that have already invested heavily are now beginning to (8)____ the rich rewards of far cleaner streets. Quieter roads and dramatically lower pollution together make ordinary neighbourhoods feel much more (9)____ to live in. Many experts now firmly (10)____ that within just two short decades, noisy petrol engines will be an increasingly rare sight in the world's largest urban centres.
sets.explain_zh: (1) climb 攀升，continue to climb 不及物动词原形；(2) popularity 受欢迎，growing popularity 名词；(3) complete 完整的，far from complete 形容词表语；(4) die 耗尽，battery will die 不及物动词（电池没电的地道说法）；(5) efficient 高效的，more efficient 形容词比较级；(6) concerns 担忧，environmental concerns 名词复数（搭配 raise concerns）；(7) clean 清洁的，clean sources 形容词修饰名词；(8) reap 收获，reap the rewards 固定搭配及物动词；(9) pleasant 宜人的，more pleasant to live in 形容词；(10) believe 相信，believe that 及物接从句。干扰项：fall(下落)与(1)语义相反；fame(名声)虽与 growing 搭配，但车受欢迎用 popularity，fame 指出名，语义不合(2)；broken(损坏的)与(3)句 far from 后需 complete；afraid(害怕的)为表语形容词，仅用于 be afraid（of/to），不作定语也非名词，与(6)所需 environmental + 名词及其余各空均不搭配，故对所有空均不可填，(6)唯一为 concerns；slowly(副词)无对应副词空。

sets.passage: The way that we now consume both films and music has changed quite dramatically in just a handful of years. Instead of buying physical discs in order to own them outright, most listeners and viewers today simply (1)____ to convenient streaming services for a small monthly fee. For that modest regular payment, ordinary subscribers immediately gain instant (2)____ to enormous libraries of films, shows, and songs. This everyday convenience is undeniably (3)____ , and it neatly explains exactly why physical sales have collapsed so sharply and so quickly. Yet outright ownership still offers something that streaming simply cannot ever (4)____ : a permanent personal copy that no distant company can quietly take away. When a platform unexpectedly loses a licence, a much-loved film may suddenly (5)____ from the catalogue overnight, without any warning at all. Loyal subscribers who have faithfully paid for many years can end up owning absolutely (6)____ to show for it. Critics also angrily note that the original creators often receive only a tiny (7)____ of the huge revenue these services generate. Supporters firmly reply that streaming has made global culture far more (8)____ than ever before, reaching wide audiences who could never possibly afford to buy everything outright. The whole debate ultimately (9)____ on what each of us values most: pure convenience or lasting control. As subscription prices slowly creep upward, some thoughtful consumers are now beginning to (10)____ whether the endless monthly subscriptions are genuinely worth the rising cost they keep paying out, month after month, for media they will never actually own or hold in their own hands.
sets.explain_zh: (1) subscribe 订阅，subscribe to 固定搭配（后有 to）；(2) access 访问权，gain access to 固定搭配名词；(3) appealing 吸引人的，is undeniably appealing 表语形容词；(4) guarantee 保证，cannot guarantee 及物动词原形（保证永久副本）；(5) vanish 消失，may vanish from 不及物动词（后有 from）；(6) nothing 什么都没有，own nothing to show for it 代词宾语；(7) share 份额，a tiny share of the revenue 名词；(8) accessible 可获取的，more accessible than ever 形容词；(9) depends 取决于，depend on 固定搭配（后有 on，三单加 s）；(10) wonder 想知道，begin to wonder whether 及物接从句。干扰项：asleep(睡着的)为表语形容词，仅用于 fall/be asleep，既非动词也不能修饰名词，与(4)所需及物动词及其余各空均不搭配，对所有空均不可填，(4)唯一为 guarantee；everything(一切)与(6)句 own...to show for it 的否定语境矛盾，需 nothing；expensive(昂贵的)与(3)句 explains why sales collapsed 的褒义吸引力语义不合；appear(出现)与(5)句 from the catalogue 的“消失”语义相反；quietly(副词)无对应副词空。

sets.passage: For ordinary internet users, cybersecurity often feels like a complicated problem meant for distant experts alone. In stark reality, the great majority of successful attacks (1)____ on simple human mistakes rather than on clever technical tricks. Cunning criminals send remarkably convincing emails that deliberately (2)____ unsuspecting victims into revealing their passwords or clicking on dangerous hidden links. Because these fraudulent messages look so genuine, even normally careful people can easily be (3)____ by them. The genuinely good news is that a few basic everyday habits can dramatically (4)____ the overall risk of ever becoming a target. First, sensible users should always choose passwords that are long and deliberately (5)____ to guess, never lazily reusing the same one across different sites. Second, switching on two-factor authentication quietly adds one valuable extra (6)____ of solid protection. Security professionals also routinely (7)____ that all software be updated the very moment a fix appears, since outdated programs contain dangerous holes that attackers actively exploit. Many people, however, stubbornly remain (8)____ , wrongly assuming they are far too unimportant to ever be attacked at all. This false sense of comfortable security is precisely what patient criminals quietly (9)____ for their own gain. By staying genuinely alert and treating every unexpected request with healthy (10)____ , ordinary users can defend themselves far better than they ever imagine. Small precautions, repeated faithfully every single day, gradually build a surprisingly strong wall against the most common online threats facing us all. In the end, basic awareness and a little healthy caution often matter far more than any expensive software a worried household could ever buy.
sets.explain_zh: (1) rely 依赖，rely on 固定搭配（后有 on，主语 attacks 复数用原形 rely）；(2) trick 欺骗，trick sb into doing 固定搭配及物动词；(3) fooled 被愚弄，be fooled by 被动；(4) reduce 减少，dramatically reduce the risk 及物动词原形；(5) hard 难的，hard to guess 形容词；(6) layer 层，an extra layer of protection 固定搭配名词；(7) insist 坚持要求，insist that sth be done 引导虚拟语气从句的不及物动词（后接 that，与被动 be updated 呼应）；(8) careless 粗心的，remain careless 表语形容词；(9) exploit 利用，criminals exploit 及物动词（宾语 this false sense）；(10) suspicion 怀疑，treat with suspicion 固定搭配名词。干扰项：saucepan(炖锅)为具体名词，既非动词也不接 on，(1)需 rely on 的不及物动词，saucepan 失败，且与其余各空语义/词性均不合，对所有空均不可填；ladder(梯子)为具体名词，(6)固定搭配为 a layer of protection（保护层），ladder of protection 不成立，且不作动词/形容词，对所有空均不可填，故(6)唯一为 layer；envelope(信封)为具体名词，(7)需 insist that... 引导虚拟语气从句的动词，envelope 不能接 that 从句，且不作形容词，对所有空均不可填，故(7)唯一为 insist；easy(容易的)与(5)句应为难猜，语义相反；carefully(副词)无对应副词空。

sets.passage: Automation is steadily and quietly changing the kinds of jobs that ordinary humans will actually do in the coming future. Sophisticated machines and clever software can now reliably (1)____ many dull routine tasks far faster and much more cheaply than any human worker ever could. As a direct result, certain familiar occupations are sadly likely to (2)____ entirely within a single generation. This unsettling prospect understandably causes a great deal of (3)____ among the millions of employees who genuinely fear losing their livelihoods. History, however, offers us a considerably more (4)____ and reassuring view of the situation. Earlier waves of new technology certainly destroyed some jobs, but they also quietly created (5)____ new ones that absolutely nobody had ever imagined beforehand. The central challenge is simply that displaced workers must somehow (6)____ valuable fresh skills in order to move smoothly into these emerging roles. Governments and large companies therefore clearly have a real duty to (7)____ generous training programmes that ease the difficult transition for everyone. Jobs that genuinely require human creativity, deep empathy, and complex judgement are far harder to (8)____ , and demand for them is widely expected to grow. Workers who learn to collaborate with machines, rather than vainly compete against them, will surely remain highly (9)____ in tomorrow's economy. The future of human employment is not at all fixed; it depends very largely on how wisely our society now chooses to (10)____ to these powerful changes. With careful planning and bold imagination, automation could yet become a willing partner rather than a frightening threat to lasting human prosperity in the decades ahead.
sets.explain_zh: (1) perform 执行，perform tasks 及物动词原形（情态动词 can 后）；(2) disappear 消失，likely to disappear 不及物动词；(3) anxiety 焦虑，causes a great deal of anxiety 不可数名词（与 a great deal of 搭配）；(4) hopeful 乐观的，a more hopeful view 形容词；(5) countless 无数的，countless new ones 形容词修饰名词；(6) acquire 获得，acquire fresh skills 及物动词原形（习得技能的地道搭配）；(7) fund 资助，fund training programmes 及物动词（have a duty to 后）；(8) automate 自动化，harder to automate 及物动词；(9) valuable 有价值的，remain highly valuable 表语形容词；(10) respond 回应，choose to respond to 不及物动词原形（后有 to）。干扰项：appear(出现)与(2)句 disappear entirely 语义相反；umbrella(雨伞)为具体名词，(3)需不可数抽象名词 anxiety 与 a great deal of 搭配，umbrella 语义不合，且不作动词/形容词，对所有空均不可填，故(3)唯一为 anxiety；hopeless(无望的)与(4)句 reassuring view 应为乐观，语义相反；balcony(阳台)为具体名词，(6)需 acquire fresh skills 的及物动词，balcony 不作动词，与各空词性/语义均不合，对所有空均不可填，故(6)唯一为 acquire；wisely(副词)无对应空，且 choose to (10) 后需动词原形 respond，wisely 已在 how wisely 中出现。

sets.passage: Wearable health technology has quietly moved from an amusing novelty to a genuine part of everyday modern life. Small clever devices strapped firmly to the wrist can now continuously (1)____ our heart rate, sleep patterns, and total daily steps with genuinely impressive accuracy. By patiently turning vague bodily feelings into hard, reliable numbers, they actively help ordinary users (2)____ subtle changes in their own bodies over many weeks and months far more closely than ever before. Doctors increasingly rely on this steady, continuous (3)____ to spot small problems that a single brief clinic visit might easily miss. An irregular heartbeat, for one telling instance, can often be (4)____ remarkably early, thereby prompting a worried patient to seek proper treatment in good time. The information these tiny gadgets quietly gather is genuinely (5)____ both for individuals and for researchers carefully studying large populations. Yet the whole trend also raises some genuinely difficult (6)____ about exactly who truly owns such deeply intimate personal information. If profit-driven insurers ever gained access, they might openly (7)____ the healthiest customers while quietly rejecting all the others. Some anxious users, meanwhile, gradually become so (8)____ on their devices that they begin to panic over every tiny fluctuation. Sensible experts therefore wisely advise treating the raw readings as helpful guidance rather than (9)____ medical truth. Used calmly and sensibly, however, these clever tools can genuinely (10)____ ordinary people to take real charge of their own long-term wellbeing and daily health. The quiet revolution now unfolding on millions of wrists is, by every measure, only just beginning to reveal its full and lasting potential.
sets.explain_zh: (1) monitor 监测，monitor heart rate 及物动词原形（设备监测生理数据的地道词，can 后）；(2) track 追踪，track subtle changes over many weeks and months 及物动词原形（追踪长期变化的地道搭配，区别于(1)的实时连续监测 monitor）；(3) data 数据，continuous data 不可数名词；(4) detected 被检测，be detected early 被动；(5) valuable 有价值的，genuinely valuable 表语形容词；(6) questions 问题，raises questions about 固定搭配名词复数；(7) favour 偏袒，favour the healthiest customers 及物动词（保险偏向健康客户）；(8) dependent 依赖的，become dependent on 固定搭配（后有 on）；(9) absolute 绝对的，absolute medical truth 形容词修饰名词；(10) empower 赋能，empower people to do 及物动词原形。干扰项：watch(看)虽近 monitor/track，但 watch 不用于设备测量生理指标，(1)(2)均需“测量/追踪”义；ceiling(天花板)为具体名词，(3)需不可数 continuous data 衔接下文 The information，ceiling 语义不合，且不作动词/形容词，对所有空均不可填，故(3)唯一为 data；answers(答案)与(6)句 raises...about 应为 questions，语义不合；asleep(睡着的)为表语形容词，仅用于 fall/be asleep，(8)需 become dependent on（依赖设备）的形容词+on，become asleep on 不成立，且不能作定语，与各空均不搭配，对所有空均不可填，故(8)唯一为 dependent；carefully(副词)无对应副词空（(4)句末 early 为副词非空）。

sets.passage: False information now spreads right across the internet far faster than the plain truth can ever hope to catch up. A single misleading social post can quietly reach many millions of people before anyone has any real time to (1)____ carefully whether it is actually accurate. Sensational, shocking claims are especially (2)____ to share widely, precisely because they instantly trigger strong raw emotions like anger or fear. Social platforms, whose powerful algorithms reward engagement above all, very often unintentionally (3)____ exactly this kind of inflammatory content. The (4)____ are genuinely serious: false rumours have already influenced national elections, harmed public health badly, and damaged many innocent reputations. Once a convincing lie firmly takes hold, it is remarkably (5)____ to correct afterwards, even when clear and solid evidence is finally presented. Psychologists patiently explain that people naturally tend to (6)____ stories that conveniently confirm what they already firmly believe. Hard-working fact-checking organisations struggle constantly to (7)____ false claims, yet they simply cannot keep pace with the relentless flood. The single most effective long-term (8)____ , thoughtful experts repeatedly argue, is to teach ordinary citizens to think critically about everything they read online. Before sharing anything, every user should pause and calmly (9)____ the original source of a story. Building this steady habit of healthy scepticism is now absolutely (10)____ if democracies are ever to function properly in a noisy age of digital confusion. The plain truth still matters enormously, but patiently defending it now demands constant, shared effort from every thoughtful person who values an honest public conversation.
sets.explain_zh: (1) verify 核实，time to verify whether 及物动词原形；(2) tempting 诱人的，especially tempting to share 表语形容词；(3) amplify 放大，amplify this content 及物动词（算法放大内容）；(4) consequences 后果，The consequences are serious 名词复数；(5) difficult 困难的，remarkably difficult to correct 形容词；(6) trust 信任，tend to trust stories 及物动词原形（信任确认己见的故事）；(7) expose 揭露，expose false claims 及物动词（揭穿虚假说法）；(8) solution 解决办法，the most effective solution 名词；(9) question 质疑，pause and question the source 及物动词原形（审视来源）；(10) essential 必不可少的，is absolutely essential 表语形容词。干扰项：easy(容易的)与(5)句应为“难纠正”，语义相反；spread(传播)与(3)句算法“放大”内容用 amplify 更准，且 spread 多为不及物或泛指传播，amplify 才强调算法加剧，故为干扰；hallway(走廊)为具体名词，(4)需 The consequences are serious（严重后果）的抽象名词，hallway 语义不合且与 are 数/义不配，又不作动词/形容词，对所有空均不可填，故(4)唯一为 consequences；saucer(茶碟)为具体名词，(9)需 question the source（审视来源）的及物动词，saucer 不作动词，与各空词性/语义均不合，对所有空均不可填，故(9)唯一为 question；rapidly(副词)无对应副词空（开头 faster 已表速度）。
